blocks.text: DTC P2176: Throttle actuator Idle position learning error

The DBW system opens and closes the throttle valve under electronic control. The system consists of a throttle actuator built into the throttle body, throttle valve and throttle position sensor 1/2, accelerator pedal position sensor 1/2, DBW relay, and PGM-FI ECU. When the accelerator pedal is operated, the accelerator pedal position sensor is driven to detect the amount of depressing the accelerator pedal. The throttle valve opening target value is set based on the amount of depressing the accelerator pedal signaled by the accelerator pedal position sensor, and the throttle actuator is driven to open and close the throttle valve to achieve the target value. The actual throttle valve opening is detected by the throttle opening sensor 1/2 mounted on the throttle body. When the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode, a signal is sent to the throttle actuator to learn the fully closed position of the throttle valve, and the throttle valve is driven to the fully closed position. If the full-close learning is not completed within a certain period of time after the ignition is set to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, or if the full-close learning value remains outside the set range for more than a set period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines that an abnormality has occurred in the DBW system and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: If any of the following conditions persists for 2.0 seconds or longer

items.text: When fully closed learning is not completed after ignition OFF (LOCK) mode.
Total closed learning value of 0.74V or more*, 1.61V or more**, or 0.49V or less*, 1.37V or less**.
Disconnection of cord (MTR2 line) between PGM-FI ECU and throttle actuator
Disconnection of code (MTR1 line) between PGM-FI ECU and throttle actuator

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
